channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Biden condemns Russia's 'outrageous' Christmas attack on Ukraine
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday denounced Russia’s latest attack on Ukraine, declaring the wave of missiles and drones “outrageous.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A baby froze to death overnight in Gaza as Israel and Hamas trade accusations of ceasefire delays
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — A baby girl froze to death overnight in Gaza, while Israel and Hamas accused each other of complicating ceasefire efforts that could wind down the 14-month war.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, TIA GOLDENBERG and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Undersea power cable linking Finland and Estonia hit by outage, prompting investigation
item.description: Finland’s prime minister says authorities are investigating an interruption in a power cable under the Baltic Sea between his country and Estonia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China and Japan agree to talks on security issues as they seek to mend ties
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China and Japan agreed Wednesday to set up talks on often contentious security issues as they seek to improve a relationship riven in recent years by a range of issues, from territorial disputes to the discharge of water from Japan's tsunami-wrecked nuclear power plant.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Azerbaijan Airlines plane headed to Russia crashes hundreds of miles off course, dozens dead
item.description: An Azerbaijan Airlines flight from the Azerbaijani capital of Baku to Russia crashed, hospitalizing survivors while dozens are feared dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia targets Ukrainian energy infrastructure on Christmas Day
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched a massive missile and drone barrage targeting Ukraine's energy infrastructure on Wednesday, striking a thermal power plant and prompting Ukrainians to take shelter in metro stations on Christmas morning.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Turkey's soaring costs are creating a 'lost generation' of kids forced to help their families get by
item.description: One-third of Turkey's children live in poverty, with many forced to work to support their struggling families.
item.dc:creator: Robert Badendieck, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Haiti gang attack on the reopening of a main hospital kills 2 reporters and a police officer
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — Two reporters were killed and several were wounded Tuesday in a gang attack in Haiti on the reopening of Port-au-Prince’s biggest public hospital, the country's online media association said. A police officer was also killed in the attack.
item.dc:creator: EVENS SANON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian ship sinks in Mediterranean after explosion
item.description: A Russian cargo ship sank Monday in the Mediterranean Sea leaving two of the 16 crew members unaccounted for, according to the country’s authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: After 20 years, the post-tsunami generation stays vigilant for future disasters
item.description: LHOKNGA, Indonesia (AP) — Qurrata Ayuni, a 28-year-old survivor of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated her hometown two decades ago, has transformed her resilience into purpose.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, ACHMAD IBRAHIM, and FADLAN SYAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Toyota to donate $1 million to Trump inauguration
item.description: President-elect Trump’s inauguration is getting $1 million from Toyota Motor North America. A spokesperson for Toyota confirmed the inauguration donation to The Hill on Tuesday. Toyota joins Ford and a number of other companies in pledging significant donations to Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Denmark to boost Greenland defense amid Trump comments
item.description: Denmark announced a major new package to boost the security of Greenland in a move the Danish defense minister called "ironic" because it came just two days after President-elect Trump called for U.S. ownership of the Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US and UK criticize Pakistani military court convictions of civilian supporters of Imran Khan
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — The United States and the United Kingdom have expressed concern over convictions imposed by Pakistani military courts to 25 civilian supporters of former Prime Minister Imran Khan over their alleged involvement in riots last year.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baby mammoth preserved for 50,000 years is unveiled in Russia's Siberia
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — The 50,000-year-old remains of a baby mammoth uncovered by melting permafrost have been unveiled to the public by researchers in Russia's Siberia region who call it the best-preserved mammoth body ever found.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN investigative team says Syria's new authorities 'very receptive' to probe of Assad war crimes
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. organization assisting in investigating the most serious crimes in Syria said Monday the country’s new authorities were “very receptive” to its request for cooperation during a just-concluded visit to Damascus, and it is preparing to deploy.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump could face renewed ISIS threat in Syria as Turkey goes after US ally
item.description: Concerns are mounting that President-elect Trump could face an ISIS resurgence in Syria as Turkey-backed forces hit U.S.-allied SDF troops in a move that could divide the coalitions abilities to fight ISIS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Magdeburg mourns Christmas market attack victims as fears swirl of deeper German social divisions
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Mourners laid flowers near the scene of the deadly Christmas market attack on Monday as investigators puzzled over the motive of the suspect and his previous encounters with authorities were scrutinized, while fears swirled that the rampage could deepen divisions in German
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Matt Petgrave, involved in play that led to Adam Johnson's death, asks for donations for legal costs
item.description: Hockey player Matt Petgrave has called the death of Adam Johnson “a tragic accident” in a public appeal for donations to his legal costs amid a police investigation.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Latvia claims to have displayed the world's first Christmas tree in 1510 adorned with artificial roses
item.description: Latvia claims to hold the title of the world's first Christmas tree, as does Estonia. Several sources cite that the first written record of a Christmas tree belongs to Latvia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: German authorities received tipoffs last year about the suspect in Christmas market attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — German authorities said they received tipoffs last year about the suspect in a car attack at a Christmas market in Magdeburg as more details emerged on Sunday about the five people killed.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PROBST and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope to skip outdoor Sunday prayer after catching cold days ahead of Christmas Eve, Day Masses
item.description: Pope Francis is suffering from a cold and will give the Sunday blessing ahead of a busy week that includes Christmas Eve and Day Masses and the start of the Holy Year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US military conducts successful airstrikes on Houthi rebel forces in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. military on Saturday said it conducted precision airstrikes on a missile storage site and a command-and-control center in Yemen against Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More than 30 dead in Brazil bus and truck collision
item.description: Over 30 people were killed after a packed bus collided with a truck in Brazil; the other passengers were transported to a local hospital and remain in critical condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a calendar rarity, Hanukkah starts this year on Christmas Day
item.description: Hanukkah, Judaism’s eight-day Festival of Lights, begins this year on Christmas Day, which has only happened four times since 1900. For some rabbis, the intersection of the two religious holidays provides an auspicious occasion for interfaith engagement.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Controversy plagued UN agency that employed Oct. 7 terrorists facing new problems as country redirects funding
item.description: UN agencies are reportedly offering themselves as replacements to scandal hit UNRWA that's been riddled with accusations of terrorism since the Hamas terror massacre in southern Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 2 dead and 60 hurt after a car drives into a German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities called a deliberate attack.
item.dc:creator: EBRAHIM NOROOZI, CHRIS STERN and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: Over 10 years after flight MH370 is believed to have crashed in the Indian Ocean, Malaysia's government has agreed to accept a "no find, no fee" proposal from a U.S. company to resume the hunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 dead, many injured after man drives into German Christmas market in suspected terror attack: report
item.description: A festive Christmas market in Madgeburg, Germany erupted into chaos after a car was driven into a group of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
